methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $15M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Bedrock Energy is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Joselyn Lai and Silviu Livescu, having raised $15M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer set of 3 private companies at the same stage and sector: Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M), alongside a broader comparison universe of 8 private frontier-tech peers and 1 public comparable.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $15M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A to next-stage advancement, yielding $30M. Peer-median triangulation across the three same-stage, same-sector comparables produces a $150M implied valuation, with the peer set ranging from $110M to $200M. These approaches reflect materially different assumptions about relative maturity and risk positioning within the cohort.

The triangulated range spans $26M (low) to $172M (high), with a median of $90M. This wide dispersion reflects the tension between stage-progression methodology, which anchors to early capital deployed, and peer benchmarking, which prices in sector momentum and relative de-risking against the comparables. Key uncertainties include the degree to which Bedrock's technology, capital efficiency, and commercial progress align with the selected peer cohort, and whether peer valuations themselves reflect sustainable pricing or sector-specific frothiness.
